reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — driven headless-Chrome run (download → real output → cached auto-init) + desktop/mobile screenshots read

rubric.evidence: Driven headless run (WASM, no GPU): fresh profile → Download clicked → state 'ready' → auto-run produced 16 entities across 11 types in 116-119 ms. Real onnx-community/biomedical-ner-all-ONNX token-classification pipeline runs in a Web Worker (models/biomedical-ner/worker.js + biomedner.js). Runnability pre-verified independently: pipeline('token-classification', 'onnx-community/biomedical-ner-all-ONNX', {dtype:'q8'}) returned 17 correct entities on a clinical sentence (diabetes→Disease_disorder, metformin→Medication, 500mg→Dosage, blood glucose→Diagnostic_procedure). No canned path.
rubric.notes: Genuine domain output. Distinct from general (bert-ner) + multilingual NER by its 43-type biomedical label set.

rubric.evidence: Live run tagged the cardiology sample exactly as expected: '63-year-old'→Age, 'man'→Sex, 'chest'→Biological_structure, 'pain'/'shortness of breath'→Sign_symptom, 'inferior myocardial infarction'→Disease_disorder, 'aspirin'/'heparin'→Medication, '300mg'→Dosage. Per-token BIO rows ({entity:'B-Disease_disorder', word, score, index}) documented in 'How the API works' and rendered in the 'See inside' strip; whole-entity spans merged from B-/I- runs with char-offset mapping.
rubric.notes: Semantic sanity confirmed on a real device run. WordPiece wobbles (e.g. 'atorvastatin' split, 'Troponin' mis-split) are honest model behaviour surfaced in the see-inside strip with confidences — exactly the point of showing the raw tags.

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id (onnx-community/biomedical-ner-all-ONNX linked, noted as ONNX of d4data/biomedical-ner-all), declares-task (token-classification), declares-backend (WebAssembly — 'no WebGPU needed'), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). Desktop screenshot confirms the at-a-glance table (DistilBERT ~65M, Apache-2.0, ~67 MB, 43 entity types, MACCROBAT corpus).
rubric.notes: Honest, complete disclosure; model id, task, quant, backend, size, license all accurate.

rubric.evidence: Fresh profile showed the honest 'This model isn't on your device yet.' download-required state + Download button — NO auto-download of the ~67 MB model. After download, a RELOAD auto-initialised straight to 'ready' with NO Download button (auto-init-on-cached verified in the same session). Output-area screenshot shows the 'Clear cached model' control + storage readout ('0 MB cached of 10737 MB'). shared-auto-init-loader (createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract end to end: absent → Download; cached-current → silent auto-init, no button. Verified both states in one driven session.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page (.err surface + Retry via the shared loader). Driven run: 'Model ready — running locally on your device.' status announced; 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests across download + reload. honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback = manual.
rubric.notes: Loading/ready/error wired accessibly; offline-cached use follows the same cache path exercised on reload.

rubric.evidence: Real controls present + driven on desktop: textarea (debounced re-tag on input), four sample-text chips (real buttons, ≈32px min), inline highlight output with type+confidence on hover, per-token see-inside strip, and a per-group entity-mix bar chart. Ladder pages add: de-identify checkbox, Analyse button (practical), window range slider + Scan button (wild), group-threshold slider + Extract button (multi-model). controls-operable=manual for a full keyboard-traversal pass.
rubric.notes: Chips bumped to ≥32px tap targets (min-block-size). A driven keyboard focus-order pass on the ladder pages is the remaining check.

rubric.evidence: Fresh visit shows the honest absent-model download state and empty output before any run; the 16 highlighted entities appear only after the real on-device forward pass. Confidence scores (many 0.99, some lower on wobbly sub-words) are the model's actual outputs.
rubric.notes: Nothing pre-baked; a disclaimer marks it a research/education demo, not a medical device.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (no content images), buttons-have-names. Status + output use role=status / aria-live=polite. Entity marks expose an accessible title (raw type + group + confidence); redaction masks carry aria-label='redacted <type>'. Colour is never the sole signal — every entity shows its text tag.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full SR pass over the token 'see inside' strip and the count bars would be a nice add.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop + mobile automated-clean (scrollWidth<=innerWidth at 1280x800 and 360x740 DPR3). Driven run confirmed mobile no-horizontal-overflow=true at 360x740. Screenshots read on both classes (desktop light+dark, mobile light): inline entity tags wrap, at-a-glance table stacks, 8-group legend + count bars reflow, Georgia display + humanist body legible, distinct WCAG-AA entity colours in light AND dark. Ladder pages use repeat(auto-fit,minmax(200px,1fr)) grids that collapse to one column on narrow.
rubric.notes: Genuine parity on both classes; support set ok/ok with the driven+screenshot evidence below.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a Web Worker (off main thread); the typing handler is debounced (350 ms). Live latency 116-119 ms per tag on WASM. inp-stays-low=manual but the architecture keeps the main thread free (only the light char-offset/merge + render runs on it).
rubric.notes: INP-friendly by construction; DistilBERT (6 layers) keeps per-tag latency low even on WASM.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap + gap and intrinsic sizing (repeat(auto-fit,minmax())); set min-inline-size:0 / overflow-wrap:anywhere on items with long unbreakable content to avoid horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — chip rows and legend use flex-wrap+gap; structured/cluster cards use repeat(auto-fit,minmax(200px,1fr)) with min-inline-size:0; .ner-out and pills use overflow-wrap:anywhere; verified no horizontal overflow at 360x740 and 1280x800.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven mobile no-overflow=true; screenshots reports at /home/paulkinlan/tmp/w16b-shots

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce dynamic updates via a single polite live region; debounce frequently-changing regions so screen-reader users aren't spammed.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — one role=status aria-live=polite status + aria-live=polite output region; the re-tag handler is debounced (350 ms). Entity marks/redaction masks carry accessible names.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region=pass; driven run announced 'Model ready'

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread / yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — token-classification inference runs in models/biomedical-ner/worker.js; only light offset-mapping + rendering on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; API section states worker use

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue-to-page navigation.

openQuestions: Full keyboard traversal on the ladder pages (practical de-identify checkbox, wild window slider, multi-model threshold slider): sensible focus order + visible focus on both classes?

The model mis-splits some multi-token drug/lab names (atorvastatin, Troponin) at the WordPiece level — is the char-offset merge robust enough that highlights never land on the wrong substring in dense text?
Are the count-bar / legend swatches (sub-24px) purely decorative, or do any need a 44px target?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent keyboard-traversal pass on biomedical-ner ladder pages (practical/wild/multi-model): confirm every control is reachable with visible focus on mobile+desktop.
Add a 'live redaction' wild demo: mask identifying spans (Age/Sex/Date/person) as the user types, entirely on-device, foregrounding the de-identification privacy story.
Multi-model composition: biomedical NER → link extracted disease/drug spans to a small on-device ontology/embedding index for concept normalisation to a coding scheme.

summary: biomedical-ner is a strong, honest domain demo: real onnx-community/biomedical-ner-all-ONNX token-classification via transformers.js in a Web Worker, tagging 43 biomedical entity types (grouped into 8 legible colour families) — distinct from the general (bert-ner) and multilingual NER by its label set. Driven headless run verified the full lifecycle: fresh Download state (no silent ~67 MB transfer) → real output (16 entities/11 types/116 ms WASM) → cached reload auto-inits with no button; 0 console errors, 0 network failures; clean mobile+desktop layout in light+dark from read screenshots. Four ladder pages (basics, practical=structure+de-identify, wild=drug-event radar, multi-model=NER→MiniLM concept grouping) each run the real model. Revision 1.
